skill.name: testing-patterns
skill.description: Vitest testing guidance for unit tests, agent workflows, API routes, and Supabase mocks.
skill.summary: Create consistent Vitest tests for services, agents, API routes, and Supabase-backed code.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, network, and weak cryptography patterns, but most hits are false positives from Markdown backticks, TypeScript examples, and localhost test URLs. The only confirmed operational concern is the instruction to run npm tests, which is normal for this skill but can execute project-defined package scripts.

low_findings.title: Project Test Command Requires Normal Execution Caution
low_findings.description: The skill recommends running npm run test. This is appropriate for Vitest guidance, but package scripts are controlled by the target project and can execute arbitrary local commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present and semantically relevant to the skill. Risk is limited because it is a standard test command, not hidden execution or malicious automation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code and TypeScript code fence boundaries. They do not execute commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown formatting or inline documentation. No Ruby backtick execution context or runtime shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Localhost URL Used For API Test Construction
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is http://localhost:3008/api/my-endpoint inside a NextRequest test example. It does not send data to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is clearly localhost and appears only as a request object in an API route test. No evidence of outbound network exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Test Words
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match words such as description, describe, and import statements in TypeScript examples. No DES, MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context contains test descriptions and imports, not cryptographic APIs. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Test Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to run npm run test. This is expected for testing workflows but should be executed only in trusted project workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented. It is not malicious, but it is still an external command that executes project-defined scripts.

content.user_title: Test Vitest Apps with Supabase Mocks
content.value_statement: Modern TypeScript apps need reliable tests across services, agents, and API routes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code reusable Vitest patterns with Supabase mocks.
actual_capabilities: Shows a Vitest unit test structure with setup, assertions, and error handling.
Provides an agent test pattern for asynchronous task processing.
Demonstrates Supabase client mocking with vi.mock and reusable mock functions.
Includes a Next.js API route test pattern using NextRequest.
Defines coverage expectations for unit, integration, and E2E test layers.
limitations: It provides patterns and examples, not a full test runner configuration.
It assumes Vitest and TypeScript are already available in the project.
Supabase examples are simplified and may need project-specific mock behavior.
It does not cover browser automation details beyond naming Playwright for E2E tests.

use_cases.title: Add service unit tests
use_cases.description: Create consistent Vitest coverage for services, helpers, and utility functions.

use_cases.title: Test background agents
use_cases.description: Validate task-processing agents with mocked dependencies and asynchronous expectations.

use_cases.title: Mock Supabase in API tests
use_cases.description: Build API route tests that isolate database behavior through controlled Supabase mocks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic unit test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to write a Vitest unit test for this service. Include setup, a success case, and an error case.

prompt_templates.title: Add Supabase mocks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to mock Supabase for this module. Keep the mock reusable and verify the expected query behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Test an API route
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to create Vitest coverage for this Next.js API route. Include missing parameter and successful response cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design a full test plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to design unit, integration, and E2E tests for this feature. Identify mocks, assertions, and pass criteria.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for tests for a service method that transforms input and rejects invalid values.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines a Vitest suite with setup, a normal processing case, and an invalid input case that expects an error.

output_examples.input: A team needs to test an agent that reads tasks and writes results through Supabase.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes a mocked Supabase client, controlled task data, and assertions for successful task processing.

output_examples.input: A Next.js route should return a validation error when a workspace identifier is missing.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes an API route test that builds a request, calls the handler, and checks the response status and error message.

best_practices: Keep mocks close to the behavior being tested and reset them before each test.
Cover success paths, validation failures, and thrown error cases for each unit.
Run the project test command only after reviewing package scripts in untrusted repositories.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on live Supabase services for unit tests that should be isolated.
Do not test only successful paths while skipping validation and error handling.
Do not accept failing tests as complete coverage for a new feature.

faq.question: Does this skill configure Vitest for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides testing patterns and examples for projects that already use or plan to use Vitest.

faq.question: Can it test Supabase code without a real database?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows mock patterns for the Supabase client so tests can run without live database access.

faq.question: Does it support Next.js API route tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an API route pattern that uses NextRequest and response assertions.

faq.question: Is this only for unit tests?
faq.answer: No. It covers unit tests, integration tests, agent workflow tests, and E2E coverage expectations.

faq.question: What should I check before running npm run test?
faq.answer: Review the project package scripts first, especially when working with an unfamiliar repository.

faq.question: Can Claude Code and Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
